findings.title: Defender for Cloud enabled on subscriptions
findings.recommendation: Enable Defender for Cloud Standard tier on all subscriptions.
remediation_steps: Enable via: az security pricing create --name default --tier Standard

findings.title: Configure Service Health Alerts (Subscriptions)
findings.recommendation: Service health gives a personalized health view of Azure services and regions used, offering the best place for notifications on outages, planned maintenance, and health advisories by knowing the services used.

findings.title: Ensure that storage accounts are zone or region redundant (storageAccounts)
findings.recommendation: Redundancy ensures storage accounts meet availability and durability targets amidst failures, weighing lower costs against higher availability. Locally redundant storage offers the least durability at the lowest cost.

findings.title: Use Standard SKU and Zone-Redundant IPs when applicable (publicIPAddresses)
findings.recommendation: Public IP addresses in Azure can be of standard SKU, available as non-zonal, zonal, or zone-redundant. Zone-redundant IPs are accessible across all zones, resisting any single zone failure, thereby providing higher resilience.

findings.title: Run production workloads on two or more VMs using VMSS Flex (virtualMachines)
findings.recommendation: Production VM workloads should be deployed on multiple VMs and grouped in a VMSS Flex instance to intelligently distribute across the platform, minimizing the impact of platform faults and updates.

findings.title: Deploy VMs across Availability Zones (virtualMachines)
findings.recommendation: Azure Availability Zones, within each Azure region, are tolerant to local failures, protecting applications and data against unlikely Datacenter failures by being physically separate.

findings.title: Mission Critical Workloads should consider using Premium or Ultra Disks (virtualMachines)
findings.recommendation: Compared to Standard HDD and SSD, Premium SSD, SSD v2, and Ultra Disks offer improved performance, configurability, and higher single-instance VM uptime SLAs. The lowest SLA of all disks on a VM applies, so it is best to use Premium or Ultra Disks for the highest uptime SLA.

findings.title: No budget resources found
findings.recommendation: Create budget resources with 80/100/120% forecast alerts.
remediation_steps: Deploy budget resource via IaC with parameterized thresholds

findings.title: Azure Advisor cost recommendations pending
findings.recommendation: Review and act on Azure Advisor cost recommendations to reduce waste.

evidence.name: Consider virtual machine reserved instance to save over the on-demand costs
Right-size or shutdown underutilized virtual machines

remediation_steps: Review recommendations: az advisor recommendation list --category Cost
Implement or dismiss each recommendation

findings.title: Resource groups missing CostCenter tag
findings.recommendation: Apply CostCenter tag to all resource groups for cost allocation and chargeback.

tags.required: do not create if no tag

remediation_steps: Tag resource group: az tag update --resource-id <rg-id> --operation merge --tags CostCenter=<value>

findings.title: Long-running VMs without Reserved Instances
findings.recommendation: Purchase Reserved Instances for VMs running 24/7 to save up to 72% vs pay-as-you-go.

evidence.name: Consider virtual machine reserved instance to save over the on-demand costs

remediation_steps: Review Advisor RI recommendations and purchase via Azure portal or API

findings.title: No hub VNet or firewall detected
findings.recommendation: Deploy a hub VNet with Azure Firewall for centralized network governance.
remediation_steps: Deploy connectivity landing zone with hub-spoke topology

findings.title: VMs without availability zones
findings.recommendation: Deploy VMs across availability zones for high availability.
remediation_steps: Redeploy VMs into availability zones (requires VM recreate)

findings.title: DDoS Protection Plan on hub VNet
findings.recommendation: Enable DDoS Network Protection on hub virtual networks.
remediation_steps: Create DDoS protection plan and associate with hub VNet

findings.title: Managed Identity preferred over service principal secrets
findings.recommendation: Assign managed identity to compute resources instead of using service principal secrets.
remediation_steps: Enable system-assigned or user-assigned managed identity on the resource

findings.title: Storage accounts require secure transfer (SMB encryption)
findings.recommendation: Require secure transfer and enable infrastructure encryption on storage accounts.
remediation_steps: Enable HTTPS-only and infrastructure encryption on storage accounts

findings.title: Configure Network Watcher Connection monitor (networkWatchers)
findings.recommendation: Improves monitoring for Azure and Hybrid connectivity

findings.title: Ensure Time-To-Live (TTL) is set appropriately to ensure RTOs can be met (privateDnsZones)
findings.recommendation: Azure Private DNS allows the Time-To-Live (TTL) for record sets in the zone to be set to a value between 1 and 2147483647 seconds. You should ensure that the TTL for the DNS record sets in your DNS Zones are set appropriately to meet your RTO targets.

findings.title: Enable Virtual Network Flow Logs (virtualNetworks)
findings.recommendation: Improves monitoring and security for Azure and Hybrid connectivity

findings.title: Use NAT gateway for outbound connectivity to avoid SNAT Exhaustion (publicIPAddresses)
findings.recommendation: Prevent connectivity failures due to SNAT port exhaustion by employing NAT gateway for outbound traffic from virtual networks, ensuring dynamic scaling and secure internet connections.

evidence.name: gh-runner-pip
evidence.param1: Migrate from instance IP to NAT Gateway

findings.title: Replicate VMs using Azure Site Recovery (virtualMachines)
findings.recommendation: Replicating Azure VMs via Site Recovery entails continuous, asynchronous disk replication to a target region. Recovery points are generated every few minutes, ensuring a Recovery Point Objective (RPO) in minutes.

findings.title: Backup VMs with Azure Backup service (virtualMachines)
findings.recommendation: Enable backups for your virtual machines with Azure Backup to secure and quickly recover your data. This service offers simple, secure, and cost-effective solutions for backing up and recovering data from the Microsoft Azure cloud.

findings.title: Orphan network interfaces (unattached)
findings.recommendation: Delete orphan NICs left behind after VM deletion.
remediation_steps: Verify NIC is not needed, then delete: az network nic delete --ids <id>

findings.title: Empty resource groups
findings.recommendation: Review and delete empty resource groups to reduce clutter and simplify governance.
remediation_steps: Verify RG is unused, then delete: az group delete --name <rg-name>

findings.title: No Cost Management scheduled exports
findings.recommendation: Configure scheduled Cost Management exports for cost data analysis and FinOps reporting.
remediation_steps: Create export: az costmanagement export create --scope <sub-id> --name daily-export

findings.title: Potentially over-provisioned VMs (B-series recommended)
findings.recommendation: Right-size over-provisioned VMs per Azure Advisor recommendations.
remediation_steps: Resize VM to recommended SKU: az vm resize --size <recommended-size>

findings.title: Resources missing required tags
findings.recommendation: Apply required tags (Environment, Owner, CostCenter) to all resource groups.

tags.required: do not create if no tag

remediation_steps: Assign tagging policy at MG scope to enforce required tags
Remediate existing resources with tagging task

findings.title: Public IP addresses detected
findings.recommendation: Review public IP addresses — remove unnecessary public exposure.
remediation_steps: Audit each public IP for necessity
Replace with Private Endpoints where possible

findings.title: Diagnostic settings on security-critical resources
findings.recommendation: Configure diagnostic settings on Key Vaults, NSGs, and Firewalls to ship logs to Log Analytics.
remediation_steps: Create diagnostic setting: az monitor diagnostic-settings create --resource {id} --workspace {law-id}

findings.title: JIT VM access enabled for management
findings.recommendation: Enable Just-In-Time VM access to reduce attack surface for management ports.
remediation_steps: Configure JIT access in Defender for Cloud for VMs requiring management access

findings.title: Activity log exported to Log Analytics
findings.recommendation: Export Activity Log to Log Analytics workspace for audit and threat detection.
remediation_steps: Create subscription-level diagnostic setting to ship Activity Log to LAW

findings.title: Monitor changes in Network Security Groups with Azure Monitor (networkSecurityGroups)
findings.recommendation: Create Alerts with Azure Monitor for operations like creating or updating Network Security Group rules to catch unauthorized/undesired changes to resources and spot attempts to bypass firewalls or access resources from the outside.

findings.title: Deploy Network Watcher in all regions where you have networking services (networkWatchers)
findings.recommendation: Azure Network Watcher offers tools for monitoring, diagnosing, viewing metrics, and managing logs for IaaS resources. It helps maintain the health of VMs, VNets, application gateways, load balancers, but not for PaaS or Web analytics.

findings.title: When AccelNet is enabled, you must manually update the GuestOS NIC driver (virtualMachines)
findings.recommendation: When Accelerated Networking is enabled, the default Azure VNet interface in GuestOS is swapped for a Mellanox, and its driver comes from a 3rd party. Marketplace images have the latest Mellanox drivers, but post-deployment, updating the driver is the user's responsibility.

evidence.name: vm-github-runner-01
evidence.param1: This machine has AccelNet enabled, but it is not possible to confirm if the GuestOS drivers are Up-to-Date.

findings.title: Enable VM Insights (virtualMachines)
findings.recommendation: VM Insights monitors VM and scale set performance, health, running processes, and dependencies. It enhances the predictability of application performance and availability by pinpointing performance bottlenecks and network issues, and it clarifies if problems are related to other dependencies.

findings.title: Storage accounts using legacy SKU
findings.recommendation: Evaluate if ZRS or GRS replication is appropriate for production workloads.
remediation_steps: Assess replication needs per workload; upgrade to ZRS/GRS where needed
